channel.title: NPR Topics: Religion
channel.description: NPR's stories on U.S. and world religion, spirituality, ethics, and moral issues affecting society and culture. Subscribe to NPR Religion RSS feeds.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: For the first time in more than 1,400 years, Church of England gets a woman leader
item.description: A new archbishop of Canterbury has been installed in a historic ceremony. Sarah Mullally is the 106th person to hold the job, and the first woman.
item.content:encoded:
A new archbishop of Canterbury has been installed in a historic ceremony. Sarah Mullally is the 106th person to hold the job, and the first woman.

(Image credit: Alastair Grant)
item.dc:creator: Robbie Griffiths

item.title: For the first time, the Anglican Communion will be led by a woman. Here's how women are celebrating
item.description: With the installation of the first female archbishop of Canterbury, women clergy in the Episcopal Church reflect on the struggle toward women's ordination and the future of women in ministry.
item.content:encoded:
With the installation of the first female archbishop of Canterbury, women clergy in the Episcopal Church reflect on the struggle toward women's ordination and the future of women in ministry.

(Image credit: Dan Kitwood)
item.dc:creator: Jason DeRose

item.title: 'Everybody was wearing black.' How the Iranian diaspora is observing Nowruz amid war
item.description: Nowruz celebrates the arrival of spring and rebirth. But for many in the Iranian diaspora, this year is different. As the war continues, many are trying to balance the joy of the holiday with grief.
item.content:encoded:
haft sin — a traditional table set for Nowruz featuring symbolic objects.'/>

Nowruz celebrates the arrival of spring and rebirth. But for many in the Iranian diaspora, this year is different. As the war continues, many are trying to balance the joy of the holiday with grief.

(Image credit: Sarah Ventre)
item.dc:creator: Sarah Ventre

item.title: Mamdani put Ramadan at the center of NYC's cultural life, bringing joy -- and a backlash
item.description: NYC Mayor Mamdani observed Ramadan publicly at a time when many politicians and activists on the right are voicing hostility and in some cases open bigotry toward American Muslims.
item.content:encoded:
NYC Mayor Mamdani observed Ramadan publicly at a time when many politicians and activists on the right are voicing hostility and in some cases open bigotry toward American Muslims.

(Image credit: Brian Mann)
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann

item.title: What role do politics play in increased anti-Muslim rhetoric?
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Maya Berry of the Arab American Institute about the role of politics in heightened anti-Muslim speech in the U.S.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Maya Berry of the Arab American Institute about the role of politics in heightened anti-Muslim speech in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Young Texas Muslims under political pressure find solace in Ramadan and community
item.description: Texas Republicans have invoked fear of what they call "sharia law" in rhetoric and legal actions against Islamic groups. For many young Muslims, that's at odds with the welcoming Texas they call home.
item.content:encoded: Texas Republicans have invoked fear of what they call "sharia law" in rhetoric and legal actions against Islamic groups. For many young Muslims, that's at odds with the welcoming Texas they call home.
item.dc:creator: Natalie Weber

item.title: An exploration of why numerous faith traditions practice fasting
item.description: Christians are in the season of Lent. Muslims in the holy month of Ramadan. Fasting is a part of both faiths' observances. Other religions include periods of fasting as well. What accounts for fasting's longevity across religious traditions?
item.content:encoded: Christians are in the season of Lent. Muslims in the holy month of Ramadan. Fasting is a part of both faiths' observances. Other religions include periods of fasting as well. What accounts for fasting's longevity across religious traditions?
item.dc:creator: Deena Prichep

item.title: Israel marks somber Purim under Iranian missile fire
item.description: Israeli Jews are celebrating Purim and drawing parallels with the war in Iran and the ancient biblical story.
item.content:encoded: Israeli Jews are celebrating Purim and drawing parallels with the war in Iran and the ancient biblical story.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin

item.title: Shia protests erupt over the assassination of Iran's supreme leader
item.description: Shia communities in South Asia erupted in anger as news emerged of the assassination of the Iranian supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.
item.content:encoded: Shia communities in South Asia erupted in anger as news emerged of the assassination of the Iranian supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.
item.dc:creator: Diaa Hadid

item.title: Chicagoans pay respects to Jesse Jackson as cross-country memorial services begin
item.description: Memorial services for the Rev. Jesse Jackson Sr. to honor his long civil rights legacy begin in Chicago. Events will also take place in Washington, D.C., and South Carolina, where he was born and began his activism.
item.content:encoded:
Memorial services for the Rev. Jesse Jackson Sr. to honor his long civil rights legacy begin in Chicago. Events will also take place in Washington, D.C., and South Carolina, where he was born and began his activism.

(Image credit: Nam Y. Huh)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
